The IX Center has hosted the Cleveland Auto Show and Christmas Connection for decades. Brook Park residents are particularly vocal about noise pollution and utility price increases.
objective: 2000-signature petition opposing Ohio Industrial Realty Group data center

project_name: Cleveland Cliffs Steel Plant Data Center
summary: A developer attempted to convert the Cleveland Cliffs steel plant on 66 acres of Plymouth Township's riverfront corridor into a data center for undisclosed Big Tech tenants. Over 1,000 people signed a petition opposing the project. The application was abruptly withdrawn in November 2025 due to legal issues regarding the developer's standing.
objective: Petition to block Brian O'Neill (developer) data center in Conshohocken /

summary: Lackawanna County Commissioner Bill Gaughan asked Governor Shapiro and the PA General Assembly for a three-year statewide moratorium on new large-scale data center approvals. The county alone has at least 11 proposed data center campuses, six in Archbald. State Senator Rosemary Brown announced support for a moratorium and unveiled a legislative package to restrict data centers to industrial zones. State Representatives Kyle Mullins and Kyle Donahue also back the legislation.
objective: Propose data centers moratorium after 1778 Rich Pike proposal

summary: The $7B Stargate datacenter in Saline Township faces fierce opposition from residents and environmental groups. Over 5,000 public comments were submitted to the Michigan Public Service Commission, with the vast majority opposed. Residents call the project 'uniquely evil' for destroying wetlands, straining the grid, and reshaping their farming community. Legal challenges allege Open Meetings Act violations in the settlement that allowed the project to proceed. DTE's 1.4GW power contract was approved 3-0 despite opposition.
objective: Sue over open meeting violations in OpenAI / Oracle approval

company: Franks Investment Company LLC / SAC III Acquisition Co
summary: Franks Investment Company LLC / SAC III Acquisition Co proposed a 2.8 million sq ft data center at the Resilient Technology Park (7340 Greenwood Road) in West Shreveport. Documents show the facility would use 7.5 million gallons of potable water per day. The Shreveport Metropolitan Planning Commission denied the special use permit 4-4 in December 2025. The City Council overturned the denial. Residents filed a lawsuit accusing the city of violating constitutional obligations, noting the operator's identity remains unknown to the public.
objective: Legal challenge to Franks Investment Company data center in Shreveport

summary: Augusta Township residents near Milan, Ohio created a Change.org petition to stop a proposed data center from being approved in their community.
objective: Petition to block Thor Equities data center in Milan

summary: A proposal to convert the former Pennhurst State School site in Chester County into a massive data center drew 12,000+ petition signatures. The property borders private homes, the Southeastern Veterans Center, and the Schuylkill River Trail. In February 2026, the East Vincent Planning Commission voted unanimously to recommend denial, finding the proposal technically deficient and non-compliant with the zoning ordinance. The developer (Penn Hurst Holdings DE LLC) did not attend the meeting. A conditional-use hearing before the Board of Supervisors is scheduled for March 16, 2026. March 16 hearing postponed due to severe weather. Developers expanded plans from 1.3M to 1.9M sq ft one business day before. Rescheduled to April 20.
opposition_groups: Prevent East Vincent Data Center Development
objective: Deny Penn Hurst Holdings DE data center on 125 acres in East Vincent

summary: Change.org petition with 216 signatures opposing data centers in Temple. Rowan Digital Infrastructure received unanimous city council approval for a $700 million, 300 MW hyperscale data center campus on 700 acres along Bob White Road. Construction started fall 2025 with 12-18 month timeline. The facility borders Robinson Family Farm. Rowan says it will use a closed-loop cooling system with minimal water consumption (1-2 million gallons filled once, lasting 10-15 years). Meta also previously paused an $800 million data center project in Temple in 2022. Community concerns center on energy consumption and infrastructure impact.
objective: 216-signature petition opposing Rowan Digital Infrastructure data center

summary: Gordon County enacted a preemptive data center moratorium, despite no active data center proposals. County officials studied ordinances in Jackson, Spalding, and Wayne counties to develop regulations for the design, construction, operation, and remediation of data centers. Officials said the goal is to ensure that 'when data centers come, we will get the benefits of them, but not the burdens.' The county near Calhoun determined it needs specific regulations before any data center development can proceed.
objective: Pause data centers in Gordon County pending regulations

summary: Albemarle County proposed a zoning overlay for data centers that would designate five areas as appropriate. The Piedmont Environmental Council raised concerns. The county paused the process in October 2025 to evaluate potential legislation from the 2026 Virginia General Assembly session.
objective: Create data center overlay zone in Albemarle County

summary: Jordan Harmon and Mackenzie Roberts (both Muscogee Nation citizens) organized town halls after plans emerged to build a massive AI data center on 5,500 acres of Muscogee Nation land (the Looped Square Ranch near Okmulgee) originally purchased for food security, hunting, and fishing. Elected officials were required to sign NDAs preventing them from sharing project details with constituents. The Muscogee Nation National Council voted 4-11 to reject the proposal (NCA 25-077) on November 15, 2025. Featured on the cover of TIME magazine in February 2026.
objective: Deny Undisclosed data center on 5500 acres in Muscogee

summary: Google confirmed a $4B data center on 1,178 acres ('Project Pyramid'). Critics raised concerns about water, noise, utility rate increases, and that 1,100 acres support only 300 jobs. Arkansas Act 373 allows utilities to pass infrastructure costs to ratepayers.
objective: Oppose Google $4B data center over water and rate impacts

summary: Dundee Township enacted a 90-day moratorium on data center development in October 2025, later extended. The moratorium freezes all data center applications including those in progress. Cloverleaf Infrastructure retracted its pre-development agreement for a data center on a 750-acre site after public outcry. At a meeting in the Dundee Village office, dozens protested with the Village president stating that people do not want it.
opposition_groups: Citizens Against a Dundee Data Center
objective: Impose 90-day data centers moratorium in Dundee Township (Cloverleaf Infrastructure)

summary: The City of Bristol enacted a 2-year moratorium on data processing centers in October 2025, passing unanimously on both readings (Sep 9 and Oct 7). The city had no existing zoning regulations for data centers. Part of a wave of moratoria in the Tri-Cities region of northeast Tennessee.
objective: Impose 2-year data centers moratorium in City of Bristol

summary: Microsoft's cancellation of the 244-acre Caledonia data center (Project Nova) is confirmed. The decision followed a 2,000-signature petition and strong community opposition. Microsoft said it remains committed to the region and Racine County officials are working to identify an alternative site, possibly still in Caledonia. Microsoft's other WI projects (Mount Pleasant, Kenosha) are unaffected.
objective: Petition to block Microsoft data center in Village of Caledonia

opposition_groups: No Data Centers in Ozaukee County
summary: Residents in Fredonia and Saukville formed a joint coalition to oppose new power-line routes serving the Vantage Data Center planned for Port Washington. The 'Responsible Energy Alliance' (~500 members) and the 'Protect Fredonia Coalition' (chaired by Pastor Patti Plough) are fighting against American Transmission Company's proposed routes of 96-123 miles of new transmission lines and up to 5 new substations. ATC submitted its application to the Wisconsin Public Service Commission. The PSC will decide on routes in spring 2026.
objective: Commission legislative study on data center impacts in WI

summary: Directs CPUC to assess whether investor-owned utility costs from new data center loads result in cost shifts to other retail customers. Report due to Legislature by January 1, 2027. Signed by Governor Oct 11, 2025 (Chapter 647, Statutes of 2025). Study-only approach; earlier prescriptive provisions were removed.
objective: Prevent data center cost pass-through to residential rates in CA

summary: Thomas A. Hobbs Living Trust requested rezoning of a 138-acre site along Joe Prather Highway (25025 Joe Prather Hwy, Ekron) from Agricultural/Residential to Light Industrial for data center development. After a 5-hour public hearing with 150+ residents and a 2,800-signature petition, the Planning Commission declined to recommend rezoning. Fiscal Court unanimously blocked the proposal and subsequently adopted a moratorium (Ordinance 2025-07) on new data center and IT infrastructure facility applications.
objective: Deny Thomas A. Hobbs Living Trust rezoning of 138 acres of farmland

summary: Manchester Township enacted a 2-year moratorium on data center development on October 14, 2025 -- one of the longest moratorium durations among Michigan communities. The township is located in Washtenaw County near several other communities that have also enacted moratoria.
opposition_groups: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan
objective: Impose 2-year data centers moratorium in Manchester Township

opposition_groups: Stop the Pine Island Data Center
summary: Google building a data center on 482 acres ('Project Skyway'). MCEA filed suit Oct 16, 2025 challenging the Alternative Urban Areawide Review, demanding a full environmental impact statement. Google's identity revealed Feb 2026.
objective: Sue for environmental review of Google project

summary: The proposed 1.8 million sq ft 'Project Loon' hyperscale data center was kept secret from the public until September 2025, despite city officials discussing initial details as early as September 2024. Hermantown changed land use plans and approved a preliminary environmental review before the public knew it was a data center. On Oct. 20, the City Council voted 4-0 to rezone 200+ acres, with nearly 300 attending and dozens testifying against. The 'Stop the Hermantown Data Center' Facebook group gained 3,500+ members. SHDC (a non-profit LLC) partnered with MCEA to appeal the city's environmental study. Residents launched lawn signs, canvassing, and door-knocking campaigns. On March 3, 2026, Google was confirmed as the company behind the controversial 1.8-million-sq-ft, 700MW data center proposal in Hermantown. Minnesota Power signed an electric service agreement with Google. The project faces a pending lawsuit from the Stop the Hermantown Data Center group. The Hermantown Area Chamber of Commerce called it 'one of the most significant private investments ever considered' in the region.
objective: Sue for environmental review of Mortenson / Harmony Group project

summary: Directs utilities to file a new tariff for large load customers (100+ MW/month) with the BPU. Gov. Murphy pocket-vetoed before leaving office in Jan. 2026. Reintroduced in new session as S731/A796.
bill_name: A5462 / S4307 - Large Load Customer Tariff
objective: Create large-load utility tariff for 100MW+ data centers (A5462)

summary: Logan County Commissioners unanimously approved a 180-day moratorium on solar, wind, data center, and battery energy storage permits on October 21, 2025. Commissioner Santomaso said developing data center and battery storage regulations is a high priority.
objective: Impose 180-day battery energy storage systems moratorium in Logan County

summary: DC BLOX built a 120MW hyperscale facility (Atlanta West) in Lithia Springs in Douglas County, adjacent to Microsoft and Google facilities. Douglas County Commission imposed a 90-day moratorium on data centers in March 2025, later extended to study impacts on local ratepayers. Despite the moratorium, DC BLOX secured $1.15B in green loan financing in August 2025 for its campus expansion. Part of a broader wave of data center ordinances sweeping through Georgia counties. Amazon also purchased 118 acres in Douglas County for a possible data center amid community concerns about sustainability.
objective: Impose 90-day data centers moratorium in Douglas County (DC BLOX)

summary: Stafford County adopted strict data center regulations including a 750-foot setback from residences, 55 dB noise cap, and 50-200 foot vegetative buffers after a 9+ hour board meeting. The county has 18 data center proposals in the pipeline with five grandfathered projects.
objective: Impose 750-foot data center setbacks in Stafford County

summary: Amazon Web Services is building a $500M data center on 243 acres along Route 3 in Stevensburg, Culpeper County, with two buildings totaling 427,000 sq ft and approximately 90MW capacity. The project attracted opposition from the Coalition to Save Culpeper and the Piedmont Environmental Council. Culpeper's elected officials have approved over 12 million square feet of new data center campuses total. A special Town Council meeting in October 2025 discussed a Minimum Revenue Agreement between STACK Infrastructure and the Town of Culpeper. Opposition groups participated in Lobby Day in Richmond in January 2026 advocating for data center industry reform. Culpeper data centers are permitted to emit up to 55 dBA at night, which opponents say is ten times louder than WHO-recommended levels.
objective: Legal challenge to Amazon data center in Culpeper County

summary: Red Wolf DCD Properties' $12B data center campus (six buildings, 1.8M sq ft, 600MW) on 400 acres in western Wyandotte County is on hold after a protest petition and lawsuit challenged the rezoning. Plaintiffs Neal Palmer and Haskell Farms LLC allege the Unified Government rushed review processes 'without sufficient study of short and long-term impacts.' The lawsuit also claims a commissioner was 'unduly pressured' to recuse himself and that Red Wolf orchestrated efforts to withdraw petition signatures. On October 29, a Wyandotte County district judge denied the government's motion to dismiss. Residents say elected officials have not been transparent about project impacts.
objective: Challenge TIF financing for Red Wolf DCD Properties data center

summary: Putnam County passed a one-year moratorium on wind, solar, data centers, and small modular nuclear reactor projects in November 2025. The moratorium was intended to give leaders time to understand long-term impacts and update zoning rules. The third Indiana county (after Marshall and White) to enact a data center moratorium. The moratoriums conflict with state policy favoring tech projects and a 2019 law granting sizable tax credits for computer equipment purchases.
objective: Halt data centers and solar in Putnam County after Undisclosed proposal

summary: Wildcat Ridge proposed a $2.1 billion, 17.2M sq ft data center campus in Archbald. Borough Council approved a zoning amendment expanding data center eligibility from 4 to 9 districts. Residents filed a land use appeal in Lackawanna County Court, alleging the ordinance was drafted at developers' expense. Three data center developers intervened to defend the zoning. On March 18-19, Archbald Borough Council underwent a leadership coup driven by data center opposition — members voted to remove the president and VP. A 7th data center proposal (Project Green, Stavola Quarry) emerged.
objective: Legal challenge to Wildcat Ridge Data Center data center in Archbald

summary: Mesh Capital LLC sought to remove a protective proffer from a 57-acre property on Cabin Field Road. Supervisors voted 5-2 to deny the request in October 2025. However, since data centers have by-right use on heavy industrial zoned properties, the developer submitted three new site plans for a scaled-back 450,000 sq ft data center that staff determined to be in compliance with the proffer.
objective: Deny Mesh Capital data center on 57 acres in Campbell County

summary: Denver-based Amp Z proposed a datacenter at the former Southland Paper Mill site near Lufkin, with 175MW of grid capacity rising to 1.1GW by 2028. A Change.org petition opposing the project received over 1,000 signatures. The Lufkin city council denied an Oncor substation permit after resident opposition. City officials emphasized the site's industrial history and zoning compatibility.
objective: Deny Amp Z 175MW data center permit in Lufkin

summary: A Change.org petition opposes high-impact data center development in Rockford, IL, demanding sustainable growth alternatives. The petition calls for open dialogue, transparent cost analysis, and investment in small businesses and workforce development rather than committing local resources to energy-intensive data centers.
objective: Petition to block Monarch Energy data center in Rockford

company: Oppidan Investment Co. / Longhorn Property Management / RR Realty Holdings
summary: Two data center developers are eyeing over 400 acres of undeveloped land in Foristell, a growing city along I-70 straddling St. Charles and Warren counties. A public hearing was delayed from Jan. 5 to Jan. 20, 2026 to find a larger venue. Resident Phyllis Elizabeth Wall brought a petition with 150+ signatures. The Change.org petition has 784 signatures. Residents cite concerns that Foristell lacks infrastructure and oversight for such development. UPDATE: The Foristell Board of Aldermen is scheduled to hold the second reading and final vote on the 216-acre land annexation on March 16, 2026. If approved, residents can file a protest petition within 14 days (requiring signatures of 5% of qualified voters). A Change.org petition has been launched. Developer: Oppidan Investment Company / Connect.
objective: 784-signature petition opposing Oppidan Investment Co. data center

opposition_groups: York County Citizens for Responsible Growth
summary: QTS is building a nine-building $1 billion campus near Lake Wylie, threatening the 95-acre Penland Christmas Tree Farm next door. Residents are concerned about noise, blinding security lights, and the sale of 400+ acres under the secretive code name 'Project COBRA' without community knowledge. Nearly 800 people signed a petition to stop the project. The tree farm owner worries increased heat could dry out his trees and ruin a nearly 60-year-old family business. Opposition group York County Citizens for Responsible Growth launched a Change.org petition with 900+ signatures. QTS held a face-to-face meeting with residents in February. York County Council has stood by the project and its 40-year incentive package.
objective: Petition to block QTS Data Centers data center in York County

company: Whitmore & Blackwell (developer) / Indiana Economic Development Corporation
summary: A Change.org petition demands that Kokomo prevent data centers from establishing in the city. Mayor Moore has publicly vouched to protect Kokomo from data centers, indicating strong local government support for the opposition position.
objective: Petition to block Whitmore & Blackwell data center in Kokomo

summary: South Strabane Township began drafting data center regulations after CNX Resources' 1,400-acre tract was marketed for data center development. 'No Data Centers' signs appeared along roads. The draft ordinance requires 300-foot setbacks, 850-foot minimum distance from residences, and regulates noise and light pollution. Planning commission tabled the ordinance in late Feb. 2026.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in South Strabane

opposition_groups: Cumberland County Residents Against Data Centers
summary: Energy Storage Solutions plans a 'NetZero' data center in Cumberland County near three churches and many neighborhoods. Despite marketing as green energy, the facility relies on natural gas combustion. The company initially claimed 500 jobs but documents show only 69 permanent positions, mostly security and janitorial. A petition gathered 1,000+ signatures. Cumberland County Board of Commissioners announced a special public forum on data center policy/zoning for March 23, 2026. The county is drafting its first formal data center zoning rules.
objective: Petition to block Energy Storage Solutions data center in Cumberland County

project_name: Tract Silver Springs / Multiple Data Centers
opposition_groups: Citizens Against Over Development in Lyon County
summary: Tract acquired 1,060 acres near Silver Springs in Lyon County to develop a datacenter park with up to 1.6GW capacity. The Lyon County Planning Commission unanimously approved the rezoning of 500+ acres. Some citizens expressed opposition citing environmental impacts and accusations of official corruption. Microsoft is also developing a 274-acre datacenter in Silver Springs. The first phase provides 150MW across 2-4 buildings.
objective: Block Tract rezoning of 1060 acres in Lyon County

summary: Takanock LLC proposed Project Baccara: 700 MW of natural gas generators and data center buildings on 160 acres near Surprise. A petition neared 5,000 signatures. The Arizona Corporation Commission approved a Certificate of Environmental Compatibility 5-0 in February 2026, but additional permits are still required.
objective: 5000-signature petition opposing Takanock data center

summary: Jonesborough enacted a 2-year moratorium on data processing centers and cryptocurrency operations, passing unanimously (first reading Oct 13, final Nov 3, 2025). Mayor Kelly Wolfe said the town was "wholly unprepared" for cryptocurrency mines. Expires Oct 13, 2027.
objective: Impose 2-year crypto mining moratorium in Jonesborough

summary: Norton City Council introduced a 180-day moratorium on data center zoning certificates after rejecting Quantum HPC's proposed 485,000 sq ft data center ('Project Triton') at the former PPG limestone mine. On October 20, council voted 5-0 to block the project after the company's founder was found to have made false statements about his education and litigation history. Quantum HPC withdrew their proposal.
objective: Propose data centers moratorium after Quantum HPC proposal

summary: Selectboard voted to pause conversations about a $5B data center on town-owned land after grassroots campaign by 'Protect Wiscasset.' Little info about end user or power source had been disclosed.
objective: Oppose Undisclosed (under NDA) data center in Wiscasset

opposition_groups: Coalition for Responsible Data Center Development
summary: Tract received approval for a 708MW hyperscale data center consisting of 12 buildings up to 80 feet tall and 2.53 million sq ft, plus an electrical substation and several hundred generators with over a million gallons of stored fuel. Residents filed a lawsuit against the City of Farmington, alleging environmental and public health harms including air quality degradation, noise, and strain on water and electricity supplies. The Coalition for Responsible Data Center Development was formed to coordinate opposition. Castle Rock Township told Farmington and the Metropolitan Council it didn't consent to the proposed zoning changes, just weeks before the city council approved the project. Tract requested a one-year extension of the plat recording deadline to May 2026. The lawsuit is in discovery with a trial potentially in summer or fall 2026.
objective: Challenge Tract air quality permits in court

summary: Johnson County Board of Supervisors unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on data center rezoning and permits in unincorporated areas on Nov. 8, 2025. The moratorium runs through November 8, 2026. The county is 3-4 months from finalizing a comprehensive ordinance addressing location, water usage, and facility size. While no firms had proposed building in Johnson County, the moratorium was adopted proactively given the regional surge in data center proposals.
objective: Impose 4 month data centers moratorium in Johnson County (Undisclosed)

summary: Tama County Board of Supervisors approved moratoriums on both data mining/centers and battery storage on November 10, 2025. Applies to all proposed facilities regardless of size, technology, or location. Typo corrections made in follow-up meetings Nov 17-18.
objective: Pause data centers in Tama County pending regulations

summary: Barberton City Council approved an indefinite moratorium on data centers, prompted by Quantum HPC's proposed data center at the former PPG limestone mine in neighboring Norton. The moratorium remains in place while Barberton completes its updated development code, which will formally define data center regulations.
objective: Impose indefinite data centers moratorium after Quantum HPC proposal

summary: Mitchell County approved moratoriums on utility-scale solar (Resolution #1326-25, Nov 12, 2025, unanimous) and data centers until Dec 31, 2026 (Resolution #1336-25, Nov 25, 2025, 4-1 vote). Supervisor Wherry was the key proponent. The county also has ongoing disputes about the Salt Creek Wind project.
objective: Pause data centers and solar in Mitchell County pending regulations

opposition_groups: Coalition Against Data Centers in South Ohio
summary: Hamilton City Council Chambers were packed on Nov. 12 with residents opposing a proposed data center requesting 240 MW of power. Resident Teri Cunningham created the Coalition Against Data Centers in South Ohio Facebook group with 600+ members. Citizens circulate petitions, create yard signs, and speak at biweekly council meetings.
objective: Petition to block Logistix data center in Hamilton Township

summary: TAC Data Centers pulled out of its option to lease 569 acres in Great Falls. The project would have demanded 600MW by 2030 — approximately 88% of what Montana's existing customers use.
objective: TAC Data Centers withdraws from 569-acre Great Falls project

opposition_groups: Butte Watchdogs for Social and Environmental Justice
summary: Nine nonprofits filed PSC complaint to stop NorthWestern Energy from shifting data center infrastructure costs to 413,000+ residential customers. NWE signed Letters of Intent with 11 data center developers for up to 1,400MW without regulatory approval. In March 2026, Earthjustice challenged PSC protective order shielding LOI details, arguing NWE hasn't shown trade secret qualification.
objective: File PSC complaint over NorthWestern shifting costs to residents

company: Atlas Power / Sabey Data Center Properties
opposition_groups: Butte Watchdogs for Social and Environmental Justice
summary: Sabey Data Centers proposed two 500,000 sq ft buildings (1M sq ft total) on over 600 acres at Montana Connections Industrial Park in Silver Bow County. A coalition of nine nonprofit groups warns the data centers consume massive amounts of water and electricity and could lead to much higher power bills for residents. Opposition cites noise, light pollution, water and power consumption, and potential residential electricity rate increases. Sabey entered an option agreement with the city-county in April, with a February deadline to close.
objective: Organize coalition against Atlas Power data center in Butte

summary: Pittsfield Township trustees voted on a 180-day moratorium on any data center proposals on November 18, 2025. Supervisor Trish Reilly noted that the township's zoning ordinance does not address data centers. There are currently no known plans to build a data center in the township, but officials enacted the moratorium preemptively.
objective: Impose 180-day data centers moratorium in Pittsfield Township

summary: Sullivan County Commissioner Jessica Means introduced a 4-month moratorium resolution on data mining and crypto mining centers on November 20, 2025. The commission deferred the vote at their December meeting pending municipal recommendations. Applies to high-intensity, third-party, revenue-generating facilities.
objective: Impose 4-month crypto mining moratorium in Sullivan County

opposition_groups: Stop the Data Centers Livingston County
Livingston County Residents for Responsible Development
summary: Township enacted a unanimous six-month moratorium on Nov. 20, 2025 after ~2 hours of public comment. Trustee Bob Wilson confirmed Meta was behind the project, estimating a $9B investment and the center occupying at least 20% of the 1,000-acre space. The group 'Stop the Data Centers Livingston County' said the moratorium as passed did not apply to the current developer. In December 2025, the developers withdrew the rezoning request, stating they wanted to 'honor the current moratorium.' FracTracker Alliance celebrated the withdrawal as a community win. Developer withdrew $1B project after resident outcry, but moratorium remains in effect while township drafts zoning rules.
objective: Halt data centers in Howell Township after Meta proposal

opposition_groups: Vicksburg Residents Against Data Center (ad hoc)
summary: Amazon announced a $3 billion data center campus near the Port of Vicksburg on Nov 20, 2025, after 2.5 years of secret development. Community forums revealed widespread concern about transparency, water usage, and environmental impacts. Named one of 2025's 'Worst Economic Development Deals.'
objective: Oppose Amazon data center over water in Vicksburg

summary: After CRG withdrew its data center proposal from St. Charles, the Festus City Council unanimously annexed 240 acres for data center development. At least two residents filed lawsuits; Wake Up Jeffco organized opposition. A door-to-door petition gathered 1,400 registered voter signatures calling for a public vote on whether to allow the data center. In February 2026, Festus City Council voted 7-0 to hire MarksNelson to negotiate terms with developer CRG. The council unanimously approved annexation of 240 acres near US Route 67 and approved rezoning. No site plan has been submitted yet and no end user identified. Two residents have filed lawsuits and the grassroots group Wake Up Jeffco is criticizing government secrecy. The city administrator said a data center operator could be identified by CRG in the first half of 2026.
objective: Challenge TIF financing for CRG data center

summary: Marshall County became the first Indiana county to pass a data center moratorium in early February 2025. Commissioner and zoning board member Stan Klotz pushed for the pause, citing concerns about development impacts observed in other areas. Following Marshall County's lead, White County adopted a moratorium in October 2025 and Putnam County in November 2025, making three Indiana counties with active moratoriums. The moratorium is intended to give leaders time to better understand long-term impacts of large data facilities and update zoning rules if necessary.
objective: Halt data centers in Marshall County after Undisclosed proposal

summary: Serverfarm acquired 135 acres through subsidiary SF ARK1 LLC, north of the city's water treatment plant on Palmer Road. Plans call for six buildings across 135 acres totaling over 2 million sq ft. Redacted load letters reference 318MW, 307MW, and a 450MW bridge phase. The project could cost $8 billion at full build-out. Residents are calling for a pause, public hearing, and stating 'Arkansas land, water, and communities are not for sale.' A Change.org petition has been launched to stop the project.
objective: Petition to block Serverfarm data center in Clarksville

summary: Amazon/AWS proposed a $4B datacenter on 471 acres in Wilmington with a 30-year, 100% property tax abatement (effective 70% abatement). Jessica Sharp organized the Facebook group 'Wilmington Residents for Responsible Development' which grew to nearly 3,000 members. WRRD demands Tier 4 EPA generators, air quality monitoring, closed-loop cooling, and a Community Benefits Agreement. Daily water demand could exceed 1 million gallons. Retired executive Kurt Cooper serves as liaison to officials. Wilmington City Council approved annexation and rezoning of ~545 acres for AWS despite continued opposition. However, separate site-plan, utility, and tax-incentive reviews are still required before construction. The site plan was tabled after city staff identified missing details on water service demands, cooling methods, noise mitigation, and pollution -- which AWS called 'proprietary information.' Wilmington Council voted 5-2 on Feb 20 to rezone 545 acres (4 parcels) from rural residential to light industrial, clearing a major procedural hurdle for AWS. A second data center by Ardent Technologies (affiliated with TAC Data Centers) got an additional 500 acres rezoned for a $6B investment. Planning Commission tabled a conditional use amendment 5-1 on March 3. WRRD (Wilmington Residents for Responsible Development) named Kurt Cooper as liaison, pushing for moratorium and planning legal challenge.
objective: Require use permit for Amazon/AWS data center in Wilmington

summary: A proposed 600 MW data center (Project West) on a 437-acre plot on the outskirts of the 3,000-person town of Hogansville has become the center of heated debate. Residents are concerned the facility would damage property values, consume massive energy and water resources, and raise utility bills. Some residents are angry that the city kept development plans secret for at least 18 months under a non-disclosure agreement signed by city council and the mayor. The project would be a $9.7 billion investment over eight years. This is a distinct community fight from the broader Troup County moratorium already in our database.
objective: Propose data centers moratorium after Fertile Ground Investments proposal

summary: Monroe County passed a data center moratorium on unincorporated land after commissioners unanimously rejected the 900-acre, $5.8 billion Bolingbroke Technology Campus. Nearly 900 residents packed the county meeting to oppose the project, citing traffic, water use, and industrialization of rural land. The property owner subsequently sued the county.
objective: Propose data centers moratorium in Monroe County (rejected)

summary: Athens-Clarke County Commission is voting March 3 on whether to extend the current emergency moratorium (set to expire March 6). Commissioners are discussing regulations including closed-loop cooling requirements, on-site clean energy for facilities over 5MW, and potentially an outright ban on data centers. Commissioner Tiffany Taylor supports an outright ban and banning NDAs for data center proposals. Environmental groups released formal recommendations for data center rules in February. Moratorium extended unanimously to June 5, 2026 at March 3 meeting. Commission voted to send text amendment proposals back to planning commission for revision. Considering Level 3 industrial threshold requirement (special use permit) for all data centers. UPDATE: Athens-Clarke County Commission voted unanimously on March 11 to extend its data center moratorium to June 5, 2026 (originally set to expire March 6). One proposed regulation would categorize data centers as Level 3 industrial, requiring a special use permit. Commissioner Melissa Link: 'We need to make it as onerous as possible.'
objective: Propose data centers moratorium after FAE Clark (end-user unknown) proposal

summary: Cloverleaf Infrastructure scrapped plans in Carlton (Kewaunee County) after local residents opposed the idea. Town Chairman David Hardtke told Cloverleaf 'if this is such a good thing for the town I want the public informational meeting and they would not do that -- it's not good for the town.' Chris Kohnle of Tisch Mills Farm Center warned 'once they take land away, it'll never come back.' The developer was subsequently also rejected in the Village of Greenleaf (Brown County). Carlton's proximity to Lake Michigan made it attractive for cooling.
objective: Oppose Cloverleaf Infrastructure data center in Town of Carlton

opposition_groups: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan
summary: Big Rapids Township passed an ordinance banning data centers in its 120-acre airport industrial park, part of a wave of at least 27 Michigan communities enacting moratoria or zoning restrictions on data center development amid concerns about grid strain and inadequate local zoning codes.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in Big Rapids Township

opposition_groups: Stop the Data Center on Taylor Lane (Facebook group)
summary: Infrakey's $10B data center proposal near Elm Mott on 520 acres of farmland would include a 925-MW gas-fired power plant. The 'Stop the data center on Taylor Lane' Facebook group has 2,500 members, and approximately 3,000 petition signatures have been collected. A Change.org petition is also active. State Rep. Curry has attended strategy meetings with the opposition. Despite major opposition, Lacy Lakeview city council approved a development agreement.
objective: Petition to block Infrakey data center in McLennan County

opposition_groups: Edgecombe Neighbors for Data Center Accountability
summary: Energy Storage Solutions proposed a $19B, 900MW data center on 300 acres of county land in Rocky Mount that would use up to 5M gallons of water/day during a severe drought. Community members wore red to the Dec 1 commissioners meeting, with nearly all speakers opposing the sale. The company claims 500 jobs but documents show only 69 permanent positions. The project may include on-site gas drilling and diesel generators, making it essentially an off-grid industrial power plant.
objective: Oppose Energy Storage Solutions data center over water in Edgecombe County

project_name: Project Matador / Trump Advanced Energy and Intelligence Campus
summary: Project Matador / Trump Advanced Energy and Intelligence Campus, proposed by Fermi America on 8,000+ acres 12 miles NE of Amarillo, received a 7.65 GW air permit from TCEQ. The air permits would allow 93 gas-fired turbines around the perimeter, making it the largest gas plant in the US if built. Authorized to release over 12,000 tons/year of regulated air pollutants (soot, ammonia, CO, VOCs) and up to 33 million tons/year of greenhouse gases — equal to ~5% of Canada's annual emissions. Over 279 comments were filed with TCEQ (mostly opposing) plus 63 contested case hearing requests. The facility would consume 2.5-5.5 million gallons/day from municipal water sources threatening the Ogallala Aquifer, with some residents estimating only five more years of water remaining. Construction paused pending TCEQ permits; a key tenant terminated its agreement in December 2025.
objective: Oppose Fermi America data center over water in Amarillo

summary: Sabey Data Centers proposed a $4B, 130-acre campus on the southwest side of Indianapolis. The Decatur Township Civic Council's executive committee unanimously voted against it. About 150 residents attended a panel with no public commenters in favor. Sabey filed for a variance of use that bypasses City-County Council vote. MDC hearing examiner recommended approval; final vote set for March 18, 2026. The Metropolitan Development Commission's hearing examiner recommended approval of Sabey's 900,000-sq-ft, 250MW data center campus on February 26, 2026, advancing the project despite ongoing opposition from nearby homeowners. Sabey committed over $5 million toward road improvements in the area. Groundbreaking expected in 2026 with buildings going live in 2028 and 2030.
objective: Rally against Sabey Data Centers data center in Decatur Township

opposition_groups: Kenoshans Unite Against Microsoft Data Center
summary: Microsoft purchased 240 acres in Kenosha County near I-94 and Route 142 in early 2025, with the data center scheduled to open in early 2026. The city signed an NDA with Microsoft in May 2024, keeping the project secret for six months. A resident's viral TikTok video triggered a wave of community opposition. Kenosha City Hall was standing-room only on Nov. 17 as residents packed Common Council chambers. Citizens called data centers 'parasites' and 'terrible neighbors.' A Change.org petition was created to stop the project.
objective: Petition to block Microsoft data center in Kenosha

summary: Janesville signed an NDA for a proposed data center, sparking resident opposition. The group SNOW determined the data center would not reliably improve quality of life. Protests held in Janesville as part of statewide opposition. Community concerns center on secrecy deals and economic claims.
objective: Rally against Viridian Acquisitions data center in Janesville

opposition_groups: Beaver Dam Education on Data Center Group
summary: Meta's $1B data center on 520 acres became controversial when it was revealed the city signed an NDA and worked with shell companies for 14 months before public announcement. Midwest Environmental Advocates filed a lawsuit challenging the environmental review. New details: the facility is expected to open in 2027. Meta has also been linked to a second potential project in the Town of Beloit via Cambrin LLC.
objective: Sue for environmental review of Meta project

summary: Gemini Capital proposed a $1.9B, 500 MW hyperscale data center on 164 acres in eastern Sioux Falls. The City Council voted 7-0 on Jan. 6, 2026 to rezone the farmland for light industrial use, despite 200+ people attending in opposition with over 5 hours of mostly negative testimony. Opponents Samantha Scarlata and Amanda Kendall formed 'Let Sioux Falls Vote' and circulated referendum petitions, but fell short with 5,012 signatures submitted vs. the required 6,704 (5% of registered voters).
objective: Force public referendum on Gemini Capital data center in Sioux Falls

project_name: Strata Expanse AI Center of Excellence
opposition_groups: Stop AI Data Centers in Lawrence County
summary: Houston-based Strata Expanse hosted a ribbon cutting for an AI data center in the Haverhill area at 1476 County Road 1A. A petition gathered 5,178 signatures opposing the project. The nearby city of Ironton passed a data center moratorium on first reading. Community members demand environmental and community impact assessments before construction proceeds.
objective: Halt data centers in Lawrence County after Strata Expanse proposal

company: City of Newport News (Economic Development Authority)
project_name: Potential Data Center near Fort Eustis
opposition_groups: Newport News Residents Against Data Centers
summary: A resident created a petition after word spread about a potential data center near Fort Eustis, gathering 3,500+ signatures. Residents rallied against the proposal. However, city officials state there are no conversations, plans, or negotiations underway to bring a data center to Newport News. The petition demonstrates pre-emptive community opposition.
objective: Petition to block City of Newport News data center in Newport News

summary: Moorestown Township Council opted out of a data center development on a 25-acre vacant site (former Pulverizer Services / New Albany Business Park Redevelopment Area) along New Albany Road. Council members decided to reassess options, stating they will pursue the 'highest and best use for the property' and re-engage in a new Request for Qualifications and Proposals process in 2026 to explore uses that serve residents and the business community.
objective: Deny New Urban data center on 25 acres in Moorestown

summary: Monroe Township Council adopted Ordinance O:02-2025 adding 'Data Centers' as a permitted use in its redevelopment plan, but residents suspect a proposed 1.6 million sq ft warehouse is actually a data center in disguise being built on the site of a local farm. Community members are urging the Pinelands Commission to reject the zoning amendment, as the Commission retains authority to approve or deny changes within the Pinelands boundary. This is part of a broader pattern identified by the Pinelands Alliance of data centers invading New Jersey through redevelopment loopholes.
objective: Restrict Hexa Builders 95-acre data center in Monroe Township

summary: Breckinridge County passed Ordinance #2025-1117A: AI Data Center Moratorium in November 2025, separate from an earlier January 2025 solar moratorium.
objective: Pause data centers and solar in Breckinridge County pending regulations

summary: Tyrone Township (Livingston County) enacted a 6-month moratorium on data center development on December 2, 2025. The township is located near Howell Township, where a major $1 billion Meta data center proposal generated significant community opposition.
objective: Impose 6-month data centers moratorium in Tyrone Township

summary: 4,500-acre, $300M solar farm near Stockton would power a Meta data center. 'Stop Solar in Stockton' Facebook group gained 2,000+ members. State Sen. Albritton filed two bills: one-year statewide moratorium on solar farm construction, and another giving county commissions regulation authority. Baldwin County hired Jacobs Engineering for third-party environmental review.
objective: Oppose Meta data center in Baldwin County

summary: Ten Mingo County residents filed a federal lawsuit against TransGas for building two data centers and two natural gas plants without adequate community input. Despite public pushback, WV DEP approved air quality permits for the energy campus. State law HB 2014 stripped local governments of authority to regulate certified data centers, leaving residents with limited recourse.
objective: Challenge TransGas air quality permits in court

opposition_groups: Starke County - No Data Center IN
summary: Starke County Planning Commission unanimously advanced a one-year moratorium on hyperscale data centers exceeding 5,000 sq ft, making it the fourth Indiana county to act. The moratorium was triggered by leaked emails from Spain-based ABEI Energy inquiring about rezoning agricultural parcels for a data center employing 4,000-6,000 people.
objective: Halt data centers in Starke County after ABEI Energy proposal

summary: IVCM is developing a $10B, 330MW AI data center on 74 acres in unincorporated Imperial County that would consume ~30% of the local utility's power. The City of Imperial filed a CEQA lawsuit; the developer counter-sued in federal court; grassroots groups NIMBY Imperial and Valle Imperial Resiste have gathered 4,000+ signatures and organized street protests.
objective: Legal challenge to Imperial Valley Computer data center in Imperial County

summary: Endeavour Energy's 560-acre, 3M sq ft, four-building data center campus south of Meta's facility. Standing-room-only public hearing; petition against the project circulated. Despite opposition, DeKalb City Council voted unanimously to approve Dec 8, 2025.
objective: Petition to block Endeavour Energy data center in DeKalb

opposition_groups: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan
summary: Green Charter Township adopted a one-year moratorium on data center development on Dec. 9, 2025, citing concerns about water consumption, energy usage, and noise. The township was among the earliest of what became at least 27 Michigan communities to enact data center moratoria, part of a statewide backlash that escalated to bipartisan legislation (HB 5594-5596) proposing a statewide construction halt until April 2027.
objective: Halt data centers in Green Charter after Undisclosed proposal

summary: St. Joseph County Council rejected a $13B datacenter rezoning 7-2 after a marathon 10-hour meeting, following an earlier unanimous unfavorable recommendation from the Area Plan Commission. Citizens for Responsible Economic Development led opposition focused on removal of prime farmland, water system impacts, and utility rate hikes. Amazon already has an $11B datacenter under construction nearby. A councilwoman claims her family was 'swatted' during the process.
objective: Deny Amazon/AWS rezoning of 1200 acres of farmland

opposition_groups: Florida Native Plant Society PBC Chapter
Stop the Palm Beach DC Project Tango
summary: Project Tango proposes the first hyperscale data center in Palm Beach County on 202 acres at the Central Park Commerce Center in Loxahatchee. The project has been scaled back to 1 million square feet from its original proposal. Palm Beach County commissioners voted 7-0 to postpone the zoning hearing to April 23, 2026 to allow for further environmental and traffic studies. Residents and environmental groups raised concerns about water usage, noise, and impacts to the Loxahatchee Slough.
objective: Deny PBA Holdings / TPA Group data center on 202 acres in Palm Beach County

summary: A $25B, 1.5 GW AI data center campus planned on 2,000 acres near Tonopah, backed by Chamath Palihapitiya. Maricopa County Board unanimously approved industrial rezoning in December 2025 despite ~200 petition signatures from rural residents concerned about water, noise, and property values. Featured in Fortune investigation.
objective: Petition to block AV Squared data center in Tonopah

summary: City Council voted 7-0 to reject a $2.5B, 150 MW AI data center by Active Infrastructure on 40 acres. Former Sen. Kyrsten Sinema, now a lobbyist, appeared to advocate, warning Trump administration would 'step in' if not approved. Her remarks intensified opposition. Emails opposing outnumbered supporters 20-to-1. Sen. Bernie Sanders congratulated residents.
objective: Deny Active Infrastructure 150MW data center permit in City of Chandler

opposition_groups: STOP Project Sail Newnan Data Center
summary: Project Sail is a 900MW, $17B proposed hyperscale data center on 832 acres in northwest Coweta County by Prologis/Atlas Development. Citizens for Rural Coweta formed in early January 2025 at Sargent Baptist Church and grew to 3,800+ members. The Stop Project Sail campaign gathered 2,245 petition signatures. Rae Lynn Butler, secretary of culture and humanities for the Muscogee Nation, opposed the project due to historic Muscogee Nation land. The project would consume up to 9 million gallons of water per day. Coweta County enacted a 180-day moratorium on data center development in May 2025 and later adopted new data center zoning regulations in December 2025 via a split vote amid public protests. As of early 2026, the rezoning hearing for Project Sail has not yet been scheduled; a community meeting for nearby residents is required first.
objective: Oppose 900MW Prologis data center on 832 acres in Coweta County

summary: DeKalb County approved a 100-day moratorium on data center applications and permits in July 2024, later extended multiple times — to October 2025, then December 2025, and finally to June 23, 2026. The board unanimously approved the latest extension, blocking new licenses or permits for data centers until that date. The Planning and Sustainability Department and Law Department are studying the effects of data centers on the health, safety, and welfare of residents. A public hearing on January 27, 2026 was scheduled to review proposed zoning text amendments establishing definitions, regulatory guidelines, and development standards for data centers. Residents have voiced strong opposition, with experts examining noise, air quality, diesel emissions, water use, stormwater impacts, heat, grid strain, and climate implications.
objective: Impose 100-day data centers moratorium in DeKalb County (PCC-DeKalb)

project_name: Bates Mill No. 3 AI Data Center
opposition_groups: Mainers Against Future AI Data Center in Lewiston
Slingshot (New England environmental health and justice organization)
summary: City Council unanimously voted down $300M AI data center. Community mobilized over a weekend to change councilors' minds within days of the proposal going public.
objective: Deny MillCompute 24MW data center permit in Lewiston

opposition_groups: Arizona Center for Law in the Public Interest
summary: After Tucson City Council unanimously rejected Project Blue in August 2025 due to vehement community opposition over water usage, Pima County approved the $3.6B data center 3-2. Amazon/AWS was reportedly the end user but backed out around December 2025 after Beale Infrastructure nixed water cooling in favor of air cooling (eliminating direct water consumption). The Arizona Center for Law in the Public Interest sued alleging open meeting law violations. AG Mayes also challenged the deal as unconstitutional rate-setting. Despite having no formal end user and significant opposition, the Pima County Board of Supervisors approved the new data center.
objective: Challenge Pima County approval of Beale Infrastructure data center

summary: Maryland General Assembly overrode Gov. Moore's veto (Senate 44-0, House 111-24) enacting a law requiring a comprehensive environmental and economic analysis of data center expansion. Report due September 2026, produced by MDE, Maryland Energy Administration, and UMD School of Business.
bill_name: HB 270 / SB 116 - Data Center Impact Analysis
objective: Require data centers to report energy consumption in MD

summary: Gates County's Board of Commissioners unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on data centers in December 2025, the first NC county to do so. The ordinance prohibits accepting, processing, or approving applications for data centers, cryptocurrency mining, or other high-impact data processing facilities until December 17, 2026. Triggered by two inquiries from PACE, a firm that promotes data center development.
objective: Halt data centers and crypto mining in Gates County after Undisclosed proposal

opposition_groups: Coalition Against Data Centers in South Ohio
summary: Waterville Township trustees unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on Dec. 17, 2025, citing concerns about electrical grid strain, water supply, and quality of life. The city of Waterville had passed its own 6-month moratorium nine days earlier. Both were prompted by data center interest spreading through northwest Ohio following Meta's Middleton Township project.
objective: Impose 12-month data centers moratorium in Waterville Township (Undisclosed)

opposition_groups: Residents United for a Healthy Lowell
summary: Franklin Lowell LLC applied to rezone 235 acres at Covenant Business Park near Grand Rapids for a data center. A public hearing was abandoned when the venue couldn't hold the opposition turnout. The City of Lowell suspended participation over unresolved water/wastewater details, and the developer withdrew its application on Dec. 18. Later reporting revealed Microsoft had signed an NDA with the township supervisor and was the undisclosed end user.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in Lowell Township

summary: Eagle Rock Partners proposed an 859-acre, 1,000-megawatt, $6 billion data center on Cooks Hill Road near the ACE Basin. The project would cover timberland and 200 acres of wetlands. Plans include 500 permanent jobs (30% IT, 30% facilities, 30% security, 10% admin). Coastal Conservation League and SELC have opposed the project. Landowners filed a lawsuit against Colleton County over the data center ordinance. State legislators from Colleton County also oppose the project. Seven state legislators (4 Republicans, 3 Democrats) from districts covering Colleton County officially came out against the project on February 5, 2026. Eagle Rock has still not filed an amended zoning application. Two lawsuits are pending.
objective: Sue EagleRock Partners over wetland and habitat destruction

summary: South Bloomfield Village Council adopted a 180-day moratorium on data center construction and a new zoning code that does not list data centers as a permitted use. The village also eliminated all areas of industrial zoning to prevent data center applications. No developer had formally approached the village, though rumors circulated. The Protect Pickaway Alliance advocacy group has been active in the area.
objective: Impose 180-day data centers moratorium in South Bloomfield

summary: Shortens review process for large-load electricity connections to 8 months. Allows utilities to bypass least-cost power demonstration requirements. Requires 15-year electric service agreement, proof of additional capacity need, payment of at least 50% of new infrastructure costs, and LED letter of support. Critics say ratepayers could cover up to 75% of data center infrastructure costs.
objective: Shield residential ratepayers from data center costs in LA

summary: Change.org petition with 1,968 signatures opposing mega data centers in Hidalgo County and the Rio Grande Valley. The primary project driving concern is Eneus Energy's RGV Data Center near Harlingen (Cameron County), which plans up to 16 data center halls requiring 1 GW of electricity and 4.6 million gallons/day of reclaimed wastewater. Eneus projects $14 billion in capital investment and 1,000 jobs. Petition started by Joshua Moroles. South Texas city officials say they have limited power to intervene since sites are outside city boundaries. Harlingen is drafting regulations in response.
objective: Restrict Eneus Energy 1000MW data center in Hidalgo County

summary: Over 100 residents protested at consecutive Vineland City Council meetings against a 300-MW Nebius/DataOne data center under construction. Opposition erupted after the council proposed a $6.2 million loan to DataOne, which residents unanimously opposed during public comment. The data center would consume more than double the city utility's 132 MW capacity. Concerns include gas-fired generation emissions, water supply strain during drought, and lack of public input. DataOne ultimately rejected the loan, and held a belated town hall at the Landis Theater in January 2026. The Pinelands Alliance called the town hall 'too late.'
objective: Cap data center water consumption in NJ

company: TPG Real Estate Partners / Aligned Data Centers / Rowan Digital Infrastructure / Amazon (AWS)
opposition_groups: Maryland Data Center Reform Coalition (Nature Forward)
summary: County Council created 2,615-acre Critical Digital Infrastructure overlay zone (CDI-OZ). Frederick County Data Center Referendum Committee gathered over 18,000 signatures (exceeding the required threshold) by mid-March 2026. If validated by the March 20 deadline, voters would decide the fate of the CDI-OZ. A legal challenge from the developer is expected.
objective: Create data center overlay zone in Frederick County

summary: Oshtemo Charter Township board discussed a preventative one-year moratorium on data center development in early 2026, despite no active proposals. The board had already enacted a moratorium on battery energy storage systems in November 2025. Neighboring Portage was also considering a parallel data center moratorium.
objective: Halt battery energy storage systems in Oshtemo Township after Undisclosed proposal

summary: Google was revealed March 17, 2026 as the company behind 'Project Cannoli,' a massive data center in Van Buren Township. Google and DTE announced an agreement for 2.7 GW of new grid resources (solar, storage, demand flexibility) and committed to a contested case hearing at the Michigan Public Service Commission. The facility would use 2-3.6 million gallons of water per day and encompass 800,000 sq ft. Panattoni is the developer. The project faces strong opposition from the community, with concerns about water usage, energy strain, and environmental impacts. The township had enacted a moratorium but Project Cannoli was grandfathered in.
objective: Rally against Panattoni data center in Van Buren Township

summary: Luther residents rallied against Beltline Energy's proposed 400-acre data center, citing utility price spikes, water supply impacts, noise, and property values. The Save Oklahoma Farms and Ranches opposition group circulated a petition calling for a moratorium on data center development. The fight is part of a broader wave of data center opposition across Oklahoma.
objective: Propose data centers moratorium after Beltline Energy proposal

summary: HB 259 establishes community benefit agreement requirements between large energy use facilities (including data centers) and municipalities. Requires facility energy rates to 'fully compensate' the utility for all costs to serve the facility, preventing cost-shifting to existing consumers. SB 250 addresses requirements for renewable energy investment, infrastructure upgrades, and community benefit agreements. The Alaska Legislature also has a Joint Legislative Task Force on AI studying data center impacts.
objective: Mandate renewable energy for data center operations in AK

summary: Rhode Island H 7331 (2026), introduced by House Speaker Pro Tempore Brian Patrick Kennedy (D-Hopkinton), would regulate data centers with electricity demand exceeding 50 MW. The bill requires such facilities to cover all costs associated with their electricity infrastructure, including planning, construction, expansion, operation and maintenance of generation, transmission and distribution facilities. Data centers would also be required to report daily water usage, cooling methods, and develop water conservation plans. The bill was referred to the House Committee on Corporations.
objective: Mandate data center water usage disclosure in RI

summary: The PA Game Commission approved the controversial land swap in a 6-3 vote in late January 2026, deeding 55 acres of State Game Lands 234 plus a 200-foot right-of-way to Limerick Town Center LLC. In exchange, the commission received 176 acres in Edgemont Township (Delaware County) and 377.2 acres in Bern Township (Berks County). The Limerick Board of Supervisors voted to send a letter opposing the swap. No formal data center application has been submitted yet, but one is widely expected.
objective: Oppose Limerick Town Center data center in Limerick Township

opposition_groups: STOP Data Centers & Rezoning in Conewago Twp.
summary: Residents of Conewago Township in York County oppose plans to rezone agricultural and residential land for AI data centers. The petition argues the use is incompatible with the surrounding area and demands the rezoning process halt until full community discussions take place.
objective: Block Elysian Partners rezoning of farmland in Conewago Township

summary: Residents of Mount Horeb have rallied opposition to proposed data center construction by local officials. The community is among several Wisconsin towns (alongside Beaver Dam, Port Washington, Janesville, and others) pushing back against data center development. Part of the broader Wisconsin opposition movement where 70% of registered voters say data center costs outweigh benefits.
objective: Rally against data center in Mount Horeb

summary: Taylor enacted a 1-year data center moratorium in January 2026. State Sen. Erika Geiss (D-Taylor) also introduced Senate Bills 761, 762, and 763 in December to bar data centers from withdrawing more than 2 million gallons of water a day for consumptive use and to create transparency standards for water and energy use.
objective: Impose 1-year data centers moratorium in City of Taylor

summary: Dorr Township (Allegan County) enacted a moratorium on data center development while the planning commission develops an ordinance to regulate the facilities, expected to take about six months. Microsoft purchased nearly 272 acres of land off 144th Avenue near US 131 in 2024 for more than $48 million, with 128 acres planned for data center development. Microsoft terminated its NDA with Dorr Township in late February 2026. More than 50 residents attended a December 18 board meeting raising concerns about traffic, electricity, and water impacts.
opposition_groups: Dorr Township MI - We The People
objective: Halt data centers in Dorr Township after Microsoft proposal

summary: Caledonia Township (Kent County) implemented a 6-month moratorium on data center and data mining operations effective January 1, 2026, running through June. The moratorium follows inquiries from data center developers about property at 68th Street and Kraft Avenue. The township is considering requiring special use permits for data centers to give the community more control over their applications and approval.
objective: Impose 6-month data centers moratorium in Caledonia Township

summary: Requires data centers to use closed-loop cooling systems. Allows municipalities, local district attorneys, and the state attorney general to sue for injunctions against non-compliant data centers.
objective: Regulate data center development in Kansas

summary: Prohibits data center fuel generation and transmission costs from being included in residential electricity rates. Commercial data centers would cover all increased fuel costs, power costs, and transmission system upgrades.
objective: Bar data center fuel and transmission costs from residential rates

summary: Encourages data centers and large load facilities to participate in demand response energy programs. Incentivizes large electricity users to shift or reduce peak usage to minimize impact on residential and non-industrial customers.
objective: Incentivize data center demand response participation in MD

summary: Reduces existing data center sales and use tax exemptions to cover only replacement equipment and repairs, rather than new construction. Significantly narrows the scope of Virginia's signature data center incentive program.
objective: Reduce scope of data center tax exemptions in VA

summary: Requires data centers to use emission-free (non-diesel) backup generators and demonstrate commitment to clean energy to qualify for sales and use tax exemptions. Passed House 61-34. Conditions incentives on high energy efficiency standards and carbon-free energy purchasing.
objective: Reform data center tax incentive requirements in VA

summary: Filed by House Speaker Kyle Hilbert. Ends data center eligibility for the state property tax exemption program (first five years free) for facilities not operational by January 1, 2027.
objective: Repeal data center tax exemptions in OK

opposition_groups: Beaver County Community Group Against Data Centers
summary: Aligned Data Centers (acquired by BlackRock/GIP for $40B) received conditional use approval from Shippingport Borough Council in January 2026 for 'Project Phoenix,' a $10B data center campus on the 660-acre former Bruce Mansfield coal plant site. The project would include 3 buildings totaling 2M sq ft powered by a planned 3.6GW natural gas power station. Separately, Fezzik Energy is pursuing data centers on former J&L Steel sites in Midland and Aliquippa, and Vistra Corp signed a 20-year nuclear power deal with Meta from the nearby Beaver Valley Power Station.
objective: Contest Aligned Data Centers approval at former nuclear plant site

opposition_groups: Chattanooga Residents Against Jailhouse Data Center (ad hoc)
summary: Urban Story Ventures proposed converting the old Hamilton County jail into 'Jailhouse Studios' with an AI data center in the basement. Over 1,000 residents signed a petition opposing the data center component, with a rally held outside. Hamilton County Commission must vote to approve the lease.
objective: Petition to block Urban Story Ventures data center in Chattanooga

summary: Repeals the retail sales and use tax exemption for refurbishment of existing computer data centers. Makes purchases of replacement server equipment ineligible for exemption. Preserves tax benefits for new facilities. Expected to bring in $140M+ every two years. Enrolled March 13, 2026. ESSB 6231 passed both chambers by end of the 2026 session on March 12. Eliminates the sales tax exemption on equipment/labor for refurbishing existing data centers while preserving the exemption for new facilities. Estimated to generate $63M this biennium and $140.5M next biennium. On Gov. Ferguson's desk awaiting signature.
bill_name: SB 6231 - Data Center Tax Exemption Repeal
objective: Repeal data center tax exemptions via SB 6231

summary: Repeals the state sales/use tax exemption for data center property and eliminates county authority to reduce or eliminate property tax on data center equipment.
bill_name: HB 560 - Data Center Tax Exemptions Repeal
objective: Repeal data center tax exemptions in MD

summary: Requires SCC certificate before data centers over 90 MW can operate. SCC issues certificate only if facility has no material adverse effect on other customers' rates, reliability, or utility's ability to meet environmental laws. Passed Senate 23-16 but House subcommittee tabled it.
objective: Prevent data center cost pass-through to residential rates in VA

summary: Laramie County commissioners unanimously approved Project Jade, a 2.7GW data center campus (scalable to 10GW) south of Cheyenne on 1,375 acres. The $50B project by Crusoe Energy and Tallgrass Energy Partners could become the largest US data center. The Hyndman Homesites Homeowners Association wrote a letter detailing concerns about deep wells contaminating the aquifer, increased traffic and noise, light pollution, gas turbine emissions, noxious odors, and wastewater pond locations. Resident Thomas White voiced opposition about noise, light pollution, traffic, and potential water contamination from deep well drilling. The project includes 2.7GW of new natural gas-fired generation.
objective: Oppose Crusoe / Tallgrass data center over water in Laramie County

opposition_groups: Residents for Rural and Agricultural Land (RURAL)
summary: Applied Digital's multi-billion dollar data center near Toronto (pop. ~200) effectively dead after the South Dakota Legislature rejected sales tax incentives (HB 1005) in March 2026. CEO told SDPB that without a sales tax exemption, South Dakota is not competitive. Project will likely relocate 1,500 feet across the border into Minnesota.
objective: Rally against Applied Digital data center in Toronto

opposition_groups: Wood County Residents Against Data Centers
summary: Meta revealed plans for an $800M, 715,000 sq ft data center on 280 acres in Middleton Township. 7 of Wood County's 19 townships have adopted temporary moratoriums, with 8 more discussing it. Residents also pushed back against fast-tracked approval of a 350 MW 'Apollo' gas plant to power the facility.
objective: Halt data centers in Wood County after Meta proposal

summary: Proposes a moratorium on AI data centers (defined as facilities requiring >100 MW for AI inference, training, simulation, or synthetic data generation) until July 1, 2030, to allow the Public Utility Commission to study impacts and recommend a regulatory framework. Sen. Bernie Sanders publicly endorsed a national moratorium. The town of Royalton voted at Town Meeting Day (March 3) to approve its own five-year moratorium on AI and crypto data centers. Vermont currently has no hyperscale data centers, making these preemptive actions.
objective: Enact statewide moratorium on crypto mining in VT

summary: Northville City Council unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers and similar industrial facilities on January 6, 2026, making it the first Southeast Michigan community to take proactive action before any proposals emerged. The pause allows proper planning for construction standards, environmental protections, and emergency preparedness.
opposition_groups: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan (EDRA of MI)
objective: Impose 12-month data centers moratorium in City of Northville

summary: Northville City Council unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers on Jan. 6, 2026, one of the first southeastern Michigan communities to act preemptively. Residents praised the proactive approach. Northville was among the earliest of what became at least 27 Michigan communities with active moratoria by early 2026.
opposition_groups: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan (EDRA of MI)
objective: Impose 12-month data centers moratorium in Northville

opposition_groups: Citizens Opposed to Data Centers in Henry County
summary: Henry County began drafting strict data center regulations including 200-foot buffers, 1,000-foot residential setbacks, noise limits of 55/45 dBA day/night, a ban on open evaporative cooling, and 30% open space preservation. First public hearing held January 6, 2026.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in Henry County

summary: Springfield Township (Oakland County) Board of Trustees approved a 180-day moratorium on data center plans on January 7, 2026, three days after planners with consulting firm Giffels Webster told the planning commission the township was 'unprepared for the growing economic pressure to construct new data centers.' A 'long-established data center development group' is interested in an 84-acre tract in the Andersonville Road area. During the moratorium, officials will determine where data centers can be located, establish construction and cybersecurity standards, protect residential areas, and require environmental and utility studies.
objective: Impose 180-day data centers moratorium in Springfield Township

summary: Amends the Federal Power Act to create 'consumer-regulated electric utilities' (CREUs) — data centers that build their own power generation fully isolated from the grid would be exempt from FERC rate regulation, reliability standards, interconnection rules, and merger approval.
objective: Exempt self-powered data centers from FERC rate regulation

summary: SoftBank subsidiary SB Energy is building and operating OpenAI's 1.2GW Stargate datacenter in Milam County, the largest AI data center project in the U.S. Ground was broken in September/October 2025, with initial facilities expected to enter service in 2026. In January 2026, OpenAI and SoftBank each invested $500M into SB Energy (totaling $1B). While no organized local opposition group has been identified specific to Milam County, broader Texas concerns about data center impacts on water, energy, and community resources continue to grow. The project is designed to minimize water usage according to the developers. Construction is creating thousands of jobs.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in Milam County

summary: Ironton City Council passed a one-year moratorium on data centers while reviewing zoning laws. The action was prompted by Strata Expanse building an AI data center in nearby Hamilton Township. Residents were blindsided by a December 2025 ribbon cutting with minimal prior notice. A Change.org petition opposing the project gathered over 5,000 signatures.
objective: Halt data centers in Ironton after Strata Expanse proposal

summary: Conservation groups (Friends of Nevada Wilderness and Basin and Range Watch) sued BLM in May 2025 to halt the Greenlink West transmission line, a 472-mile overhead power line from Las Vegas to Yerington being built largely to serve massive data center demand (4,000+ MW committed) in the Reno area. The lawsuit alleges federal environmental law violations. Construction on Greenlink West is underway with an anticipated 2027 service date, but the Beatty segment may be suspended due to Department of Defense concerns about encroachment into Nevada Test and Training Range restricted airspace. NV Energy customers are expected to bear most of the $4.2B cost for the combined Greenlink North and West projects, raising ratepayer concerns.
objective: Legal challenge to NV Energy data center in Nevada

summary: Saline City Council unanimously adopted a one-year moratorium on Jan. 12, 2026 after Mayor Brian Marl disclosed that Fortune 100 companies had contacted him about building data centers on adjacent township land. The city's zoning code had no data center provisions. Nearby, the $7B Stargate project (OpenAI/Oracle) proceeds in Saline Township on a 575-acre campus approved for 1.4 GW.
objective: Halt data centers in City of Saline after Related Digital proposal

opposition_groups: No Data Centers in Stokes County
Occaneechi Band of the Saponi Nation
summary: Project Delta by Engineered Land Solutions proposes an 1,800-acre AI data center campus in Walnut Cove near the Dan River, with 998 acres to be developed and the rest as floodplain. The campus would total 'several hundred megawatts' with a new dedicated substation. The county would collect an estimated $40 million in annual property taxes (current tax bill is under $8,000). Stokes County Board of Commissioners approved the rezoning 3-2 in January 2026 despite opposition from Clean Water for North Carolina and a local No Data Centers petition. In March 2026, the Southern Coalition for Social Justice filed a lawsuit (Hairston Clan v. Stokes County) in Stokes County Superior Court to stop the project, arguing commissioners ignored environmental and cultural impacts. The land includes sites significant to the Occaneechi Band of the Saponi Nation and historically Black farming communities. Developer (Engineered Land Solutions) responded to the SELC/SCSJ lawsuit in mid-March, but no ruling yet. Stokes County primary voters rejected pro-datacenter incumbents in March 3 vote.
objective: Ban data centers in designated zones in Stokes County

summary: Roswell City Council approved a 90-day emergency moratorium on new data centers (through April 12) as one of its first acts under a new mayor and three new council members. The Community Development Department will use the pause to create zoning and land use standards related to data centers, utility usage, and environmental issues. Roswell joins Atlanta, DeKalb County, Troup County, Social Circle, LaGrange, and Coweta County in the growing list of metro Atlanta area data center moratoriums.
objective: Impose 90-day data centers moratorium in Roswell

summary: Requires data center owners to submit monthly reports to the State Energy Commission on energy and water consumption. Prohibits disclosure of personally identifiable information or energy data for specific utility customers. Introduced by Assemblymember Bauer-Kahan.
objective: Require data center energy and water reports in CA

summary: Houston-based Cloverleaf Infrastructure offered $120,000 per acre for approximately 500 acres to build the first AI data center in northeast Wisconsin, directly across from Ledgestone Vineyards. Nearly 100 residents attended a 'Stop the Data Center' meeting, launching petitions and appeals. Cloverleaf withdrew on Jan. 13, 2026 after speaking with village officials, citing lack of local support.
objective: Legal challenge to Cloverleaf Infrastructure data center in Village of Greenleaf

summary: Madison Common Council passed a one-year moratorium on Jan 13, 2026 on new data centers and telecom centers larger than 10,000 sq ft. Separately, QTS formally withdrew its $12B data center proposal from the Village of DeForest on Jan 28, 2026 after the village determined the project was not feasible and announced its intention to deny the annexation of 1,600 acres from the Town of Vienna.
objective: Halt data centers in City of Madison after QTS Data Centers proposal

opposition_groups: GASP (Greater Birmingham Alliance to Stop Pollution)
summary: Birmingham City Council unanimously approved a 180-day moratorium on applications for new or expanding hyperscale data centers on March 3, 2026. Facilities under 20 megawatts are exempt. Two existing projects (Nebius Oxmoor AI Factory at 300MW and one other) that filed before the vote are exempt. The moratorium is now active and in effect.
objective: Impose 180-day data centers moratorium in Birmingham (Nebius)
